perturbation.original_text: "Gross Rooms Revenue" means all revenues derived from the sale or rental of Guest Rooms (both transient and permanent) of the Hotel, including revenue derived from the redemption of points or rewards under the loyalty programs in which the Hotel participates, amounts attributable to breakfast (where the guest room rate includes breakfast), and guaranteed no-show revenue and credit transactions, whether or not collected, at the actual rates charged, less allowances for any Guest Room rebates and overcharges, and will not include taxes collected directly from patrons or guests. Group booking rebates, if any, paid by you or on your behalf to third-party groups for group stays must be included in, and not deducted from, the calculation of Gross Rooms Revenue.
perturbation.changed_text: "Gross Rooms Revenue" means revenues derived from Guest Rooms. It excludes certain revenues based on internal accounting practices.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Gross Rooms Revenue" is comprehensive, detailing inclusions and exclusions. The modified definition is vague and contradicts the comprehensive list previously provided, as it makes reference to "internal accounting practices", which is not objective. This creates uncertainty in calculating fees, creating in-text contradiction to what was originally said in the full excerpt.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Calculation and Payment of Fees. 8.2.1 The Monthly Fees will be calculated in accordance with the accounting methods of the then-current Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry, or such other accounting methods specified by us in the Manual. 8.2.2 The Monthly Fees will be paid to us at the place and in the manner we designate on or before the fifteenth (15th) day of each month and will be accompanied by our standard schedule setting forth in reasonable detail the computation of the Monthly Fees for such month. 8.2.3 We may require you to transmit the Monthly Fees and all other payments required under this Agreement by wire transfer or other form of electronic funds transfer and to provide the standard schedule in electronic form. You must bear all costs of wire transfer or other form of electronic funds transfer or other electronic payment and reporting. 8.2.4 In the event of fire or other insured casualty that results in a reduction of Gross Rooms Revenue, you will determine and pay us, from the proceeds of any business interruption or other insurance applicable to loss of revenues, an amount equal to the forecasted Monthly Fees, based on the Gross Rooms Revenue amounts agreed on between you and your insurance company that would have been paid to us in the absence of such casualty.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific definition of 'Gross Rooms Revenue,' including what is and is not included. The changed text introduces a vague definition based on 'internal accounting practices.' This creates a direct contradiction because the original text specifies the components of Gross Rooms Revenue, while the changed text allows for deviation based on unspecified internal practices. This directly contradicts section 8.2, which requires calculation of fees, because the basis for calculating the fees is no longer clear and objective, opening it up to variable definitions depending on accounting practices. The modified definition creates an ambiguity that breaks consistency with the detailed calculation requirements in section 8.2, rendering those requirements potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 4.6 Manual. We will issue to you or make available in electronic form the Manual and any revisions and updates we may make to the Manual during the Term. You agree to ensure that your copy of the Manual is, at all times, current and up to date. If there is any dispute as to your compliance with the provisions of the Manual, the master copy of the Manual maintained at our principal office will control.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.6 Manual. We may make reference to a Manual from time to time. You are not required to maintain or follow the Manual.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Franchisor will provide a Manual and updates, and the Franchisee must keep it up-to-date. The modified version now states the Franchisee is not required to follow the manual. This creates uncertainty regarding which standards the Franchisee must adhere to, given the reference in other sections to the manual is expected to be followed, creating in-text contradiction to what was originally said in the full excerpt.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1.14 treat as confidential the Standards, the Manual and all other Proprietary Information. You acknowledge and agree that you do not acquire any interest in the Proprietary Information other than the right to utilize the same in the development and operation of the Hotel under the terms of this Agreement. You agree that you will not use the Proprietary Information in any business or for any purpose other than in the development and operation of the Hotel under the System and will maintain the absolute confidentiality of the Proprietary Information during and after the Term. You will not make unauthorized copies of any portion of the Proprietary Information; and will adopt and implement all procedures we may periodically establish in our business judgment to prevent unauthorized use or disclosure of the Proprietary Information, including restrictions on disclosure to employees and the use of non-disclosure and non-competition clauses in agreements with employees, agents and independent contractors who have access to the Proprietary information;
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the franchisee follow a manual, but the changed text explicitly states they are not required to follow it. This directly contradicts section 5.1.14, which requires the franchisee to treat the manual as confidential and utilize it in the operation of the Hotel. The explicit removal of the requirement to follow the manual fundamentally undermines the confidentiality and utilization requirements in section 5.1.14, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Your Transfer. You understand and acknowledge that the rights and duties in this Agreement are personal to you and that we are entering into this Agreement in reliance on your business skill, financial capacity, and the personal character of you, your officers, directors, partners, members, shareholders or trustees. A Transfer by you of any Equity Interest, or this Agreement, or any of your rights or obligations under this Agreement, or a Transfer by an Equity Owner is prohibited other than as expressly permitted herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Your Transfer. Transfers of Equity Interest is allowed under any circumstance. The rights and duties in this Agreement, is not personal to you.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly limits equity interest transfers, requiring consent. The modified text now allows it under any circumstance. Removing the restriction on equity transfers creates an in-text contradiction, creating uncertainty on whether there should be consent or not before transferring.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2.2 Permitted Transfers That Require Notice and Consent. We will permit you or any Equity Owner named in the Addendum as of the Effective Date (or any transferee Equity Owner we subsequently approve) to engage in the Permitted Transfers set forth below if any such Permitted Transfer does not result in a change of Control of the Franchisee, the Hotel or the Hotel Site and: (a) the proposed transferee is not a Sanctioned Person or a Competitor; (b) you give us at least sixty (60) days' advance written notice of the proposed Permitted Transfer (including the identity and contact information for any proposed transferee and any other information we may require in order to review the proposed Permitted Transfer); (c) you pay to us a nonrefundable processing fee of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000) with the Permitted Transfer request; (d) you follow our then-current procedure for processing Permitted Transfers; and (e) you execute any documents required by us for processing Permitted Transfers. If a Permitted Transfer listed in Subsection 13.2.2 otherwise qualifies as a Permitted Transfer without notice or consent under Subsection 13.2.1, the provisions of Subsection 13.2.1 will control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 13.2 now allows transfers of equity interest under any circumstance, contradicting Section 13.2.2 which outlines specific 'Permitted Transfers That Require Notice and Consent'. This creates a direct conflict because the original text and the contradicted text requires consent for specific equity transfers, while the changed text allows it without any restrictions, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
